mission.title: Install and Configure Devfile on Kubernetes
mission.description: Devfile is a Kube-native API for defining cloud development workspaces. Installing it allows you to manage development environments in Kubernetes using a standardized specification, enhancing productivity and collaboration.

steps.title: Add the Devfile Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Devfile Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add devfile https://devfile.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Devfile Custom Resource Definition (CRD)
steps.description:
Install the Devfile CRD using Helm. This will create the necessary resources in your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
helm install devfile devfile/devfile --namespace devfile --create-namespace --version v2.3.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Devfile pods are running correctly in the devfile namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace devfile
```

steps.title: Configure Resource Limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Devfile deployment to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/devfile --namespace devfile --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Devfile
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow access to the Devfile resources.
```bash
kubectl create role devfile-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=devworkspaces --namespace devfile
kubectl create rolebinding devfile-rolebinding --role=devfile-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace devfile
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Devfile will show the Devfile pods running in the devfile namespace, and the resource limits and RBAC configurations will be applied correctly. You can now start using the Devfile API for your development workspaces.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add devfile https://devfile.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install devfile devfile/devfile --namespace devfile --create-namespace --version v2.3.0

kubectl create role devfile-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=devworkspaces --namespace devfile
kubectl create rolebinding devfile-rolebinding --role=devfile-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace devfile

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Devfile release from the Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources created by the Helm chart.
```bash
helm uninstall devfile --namespace devfile
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and any persistent resources that were created during the installation. This ensures that all related resources are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete crd devworkspaces.devfile.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for the Devfile installation and verify that it has been removed. This step ensures that all resources are cleaned up from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace devfile
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's important to back up the current state of the Devfile resources. You can use the following command to export the current configuration.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace devfile -o yaml > devfile-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and run the upgrade command to install the latest version of the Devfile chart. Make sure to specify the version you want to upgrade to.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade devfile devfile/devfile --namespace devfile --version v2.4.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After upgrading, verify that the Devfile pods are running the new version correctly in the devfile namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace devfile
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Devfile pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the failing pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace devfile
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the Helm upgrade, check the currently installed version and ensure that the version you are trying to upgrade to is compatible.
```bash
helm list --namespace devfile
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC issues preventing access
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience access issues with Devfile resources, verify that the Role and RoleBinding were created correctly and that the service account has the necessary permissions.
```bash
kubectl get rolebinding --namespace devfile
kubectl describe rolebinding devfile-rolebinding --namespace devfile
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being killed due to OOMKilled errors, consider adjusting the resource limits set for the Devfile deployment. Check the current resource usage with:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace devfile
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
